exemplar.title: How do Bulgakov and Wilde use supernatural elements   in "Master and Margarita" and "The Picture of Dorian Gray" to explore the limits of human consciousness?

detailedExemplarData.title: How do Bulgakov and Wilde use supernatural elements   in "Master and Margarita" and "The Picture of Dorian Gray" to explore the limits of human consciousness?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Research question is clearly stated, focused, and consistently referenced.
Topic is communicated accurately with clear purpose and effective signposting through section headers.
Overall structure (introduction, themed chapters, conclusion) supports logical progression of ideas.
weaknesses: Methodology is only partially explained, with uneven justification for source selection.
In-text referencing is inconsistentâseveral sources appear only in the bibliography.
Some phrasing slips and minor inconsistencies (e.g., âSupernatural Elementâ vs âElementsâ) blur precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission demonstrates a clear, focused research question tightly connected to the analysis, and topic communication is accurate and purposeful. Methodology is mostly complete with a good range of relevant sources, but the lack of explicit justification for source selection and uneven in-text citations prevent topâband performance. Overall, the work sits solidly at the upper end of the 3â4 band for methodology and at the lower end of 5â6 for clarity and focus, yielding an overall score of 5.

strengths: Solid knowledge of both primary texts with pertinent episodes and contexts summarized.
Effective use of several secondary critics to deepen interpretation of symbolism.
Mostly accurate application of literary terminology (e.g., symbolism, allegory, Gothic).
weaknesses: Historical background sometimes lacks nuance (e.g., broad references to Stalinâs Russia).
Frequent inconsistencies in terminology and spelling (e.g., âWildâ for Wilde, character names).
Several citations are incomplete or drawn from non-scholarly websites, weakening academic rigour.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student exhibits good breadth of knowledge and generally effective use of sources, placing them at the upper end of the 3â4 band. However, recurring lapses in precision (terminology and spelling) and reliance on generic web sources limit the work from reaching the top band. A score of 4 appropriately reflects strengths in understanding alongside these limitations.

strengths: Research draws on a reasonably wide range of relevant primary and secondary materials.
Analysis moves beyond summary to link supernatural motifs with themes of consciousness and morality.
Occasional critical insights enrich the argument (e.g., linking Bulgakovâs ball to a real historical event).
weaknesses: Analytical depth is uneven; many paragraphs delay commentary with extended description.
Evaluation of sources and counter-arguments is minimal or superficial.
Conclusion largely restates earlier points without synthesizing new insights or alternative perspectives.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of Critical Thinking fall within the 4â6 band: research quality is solidly placed, analysis and discussion show room for deeper critical evaluation. The workâs strong pointsârelevant research base and occasional insightful linksâjustify placement at the top of the 4â6 band. The absence of consistently robust analysis and critical evaluation prevents movement into the 7â9 band, so a score of 6 accurately reflects the upper-band performance.

strengths: Includes all required elements (word count, title, numbered pages) and consistent section headings.
Table of contents is clear and supports navigation.
Bibliography follows a recognisable citation style.
weaknesses: Some MLA conventions are unevenly applied (hyperlinks in works-cited, inconsistent quotation formatting).
Page numbers in the table of contents do not match actual numbering.
Occasional long quotations lack proper indentation and run-on paragraphs impede clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets the descriptor for a clearly appropriate structure with most layout considerations correctly applied, placing it in the 3â4 band. Minor lapses in citation formatting, pagination mismatches, and formatting of quotations prevent topâband presentation, resulting in a score of 3.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Strengths:** Primary texts are used together with a works-cited list of c. 20 secondary sources (journal articles, encyclopedia entries).  A conventional EE structure (introduction, themed chapters, conclusion) and MLA-style in-text citations are attempted.  **Weaknesses/limitations:**  In-text referencing is uneven (several secondary sources appear only in the bibliography); little justification is given for source selection; and the method for a comparative approach is implicit rather than explained.  

These qualities fit the âmostly complete methodologyâ¦ generally relevant sourcesâ description of the 3-4 band, with the stronger side of that band meriting 4.

**Strengths:** The essay includes a word count, title, and numbered pages; headings are formatted consistently; bibliography follows a recognisable citation style.  **Weaknesses/limitations:**  Some MLA conventions are applied inconsistently, hyperlinks remain in the works-cited list, and a few long quotations lack indentation.  

Most layout features are correctly applied, satisfying the criteria for the 3-4 band; a score of 3 reflects the noted lapses.

**Strengths:** The bibliography shows a reasonable range of scholarly articles and primary texts, and most evidence cited (quotations from the novels, contextual scholarship) is relevant to the research question.  **Weaknesses/limitations:**  Some sources are popular websites (SparkNotes, Britannica) that add limited academic value, and integration into the argument is at times superficial.  

Given these mixed qualities, the research sits comfortably in the 4-6 band; a mid-band score of 5 is appropriate.

**Strengths:** The studentâs topic â the comparative use of the supernatural to probe human consciousness in two set texts â is stated prominently on the title page, repeated in the introduction, and sign-posted through section headings.  **Weaknesses/limitations:**  Minor wording slips (âSupernatural Elementâ instead of âElementsâ) and some meandering phrasing occasionally blur precision, yet the central idea remains clear to a non-specialist reader.  

Overall, the topic is communicated accurately, with a clear sense of purpose and direction that guides the reader, matching the descriptors for the 5-6 band.

**Strengths:** The discussion is logically ordered (use, symbolism, social corruption) and returns to the research question in the conclusion. Occasional evaluative comments recognise limitations of charactersâ perspectives.  **Weaknesses/limitations:**  Argumentation can be repetitive, evaluation of sources is minimal, and the conclusion largely restates earlier points without synthesising insights.  

The strand thus sits at the threshold between âadequateâ and âgood,â justifying a score of 4 in the 4-6 band.

**Strengths:** The essay moves beyond plot summary, drawing connections between supernatural motifs and themes of morality, consciousness, and social critique.  **Weaknesses/limitations:**  Analytical depth is uneven; paragraphs often rely on extended description before offering brief commentary, and counter-arguments or alternative readings are rarely examined.  Several assertions (e.g., about Soviet propaganda) lack supporting evidence.  

These features correspond to âadequateâgood analysis â¦ mostly supported,â meriting a lower-mid score of 4 within the 4-6 band.

**Strengths:** Required structural elements (title page, table of contents, section headings, works cited) are present and in logical order.  **Weaknesses/limitations:**  Pagination in the contents table does not match extract page numbers, some sections run together without clear transitions, and chapter titles occasionally overlap in focus.  

The work therefore meets the descriptor for a âclearly appropriate structure,â but minor flaws keep it to the lower end of the 3-4 band.

**Strengths:** The student shows solid knowledge of both novels, summarising key episodes (Satanâs Ball, portrait changes) and situating them in Soviet and Victorian contexts.  Secondary critics are occasionally invoked to deepen points about symbolism.  **Weaknesses/limitations:**  Historical background is sometimes superficial (e.g., references to âStalinâs Russiaâ conflated with the 1930s without nuance), and several citations are generic website summaries rather than scholarly analyses.  

The breadth and mostly accurate understanding correspond with the upper end of the 3-4 band.

**Strengths:** Literary terms such as âsymbolism,â âallegory,â âdramatic irony,â and âGothicâ are used in appropriate contexts.  **Weaknesses/limitations:**  Terminology is occasionally imprecise (e.g., calling Lord Henry âsupernaturalâ), and some concepts (intertextuality, satire) are mentioned without definition.  Spelling inconsistencies (e.g., âWildâ for Wilde) indicate uneven control.  

The work therefore demonstrates mostly accurate but not fully consistent usage, placing it in the middle of the 3-4 band; a score of 3 reflects these lapses.

**Strengths:** The research question is explicitly phrased (âHow do Bulgakov and Wilde â¦?â), narrow enough to be answered in 4 000 words, and tightly connected to the ensuing discussion of Woland, the portrait, Satanâs Ball, etc.  **Weaknesses/limitations:**  The wording âexplore the limits of human consciousnessâ is conceptually ambitious and not consistently theorised, but the scope remains manageable.  

Because the question is clearly stated, focused and consistently referenced in topic sentences, the work aligns with the 5-6 band.

annotations.comment: The table of contents is clearly structured with appropriate headings, aiding navigation.
The studentâs title communicates the research topic precisely and establishes clear purpose and focus from the outset.
The final sentence of the introduction ends abruptly; ensure full sentences to convey context clearly and maintain academic rigor.
Pages are consistently numbered in the header, satisfying pagination requirements.
This section header clearly signposts the analytical focus and supports logical structure.
Consider revising "Supernatural Element" to "Supernatural Elements" in the title to ensure consistency with the research question and text.
Word count is clearly stated and remains under the 4,000-word limit, meeting formatting requirements.
The connection between Lord Henry as a 'devilish figure' requires further explanation and evidence to strengthen analytical depth.
The NounadondÃ© citation is incomplete without publication details or page numbers; full reference will enhance source credibility.
Consider integrating counter-arguments or alternative readings to deepen analysis beyond descriptive summary.
Page numbers in the contents table do not match the actual page numbering; verify and correct to improve reader guidance.
The quote from Eli Roth is effective but would benefit from integration into your argument and a complete reference to strengthen scholarly context.
Terminology around supernatural elements is inconsistently applied (e.g., uppercase/lowercase); maintain consistent usage for clarity.
Reliance on Litcharts as a source limits academic rigor; replace with peer-reviewed criticism for stronger evidence.
The Collins definition of 'supernatural elements' lacks full bibliographic detail; include author and year in the works cited for credibility.
Paragraph contains rich description but would benefit from earlier analytical commentary to support interpretive claims promptly.
Oscar Wildeâs name is misspelled as 'Wild' in multiple instances; correct this to demonstrate precision and respect for authorial names.
The new section on societal corruption is promising; ensure balanced comparisons between the two texts to strengthen coherence.
This introduction to Dorian and Woland repeats earlier points; vary language and focus on advancing the argument.
The discussion of Dorianâs portrait transformation ends abruptly; expand on how this symbolism uniquely contributes to your research question.
The inclusion of a clear 'Conclusion' section effectively signals closure and meets structural expectations.
The conclusion restates earlier points without synthesizing deeper insights; aim to articulate new understanding or implications.
Again, 'Wild' appears instead of 'Wilde'; maintaining correct authorial names is essential for scholarly precision.
This paragraph leans toward generic summary; incorporate critical evaluation of how supernatural elements function differently in each text.
The contextual linking of Bulgakovâs ball to the real American embassy event enriches your argument with solid historical research.
The long sentence on allegorical importance blends multiple ideas; consider splitting into clearer, focused statements.
Ensure consistency in spelling character names (e.g., Likhodeev) to maintain accuracy in interpretation.
This section is overly verbose, which can obscure your main message; focus on essential arguments for coherence.
Multiple entries in the works-cited list are from non-peer-reviewed websites; prioritize scholarly sources for academic credibility.
The conclusion spans across multiple paragraphs without clear new analysis; streamline to reinforce key findings concisely.
Excessive reliance on general websites (e.g., blogs, wikis) dilutes the academic strength; reduce or replace with academic journals.
Long URLs break citation lines; consider using shortened DOIs or standardized citation formatting to enhance readability.
Citation formatting varies across entries (indentation, URL inclusion); apply one style consistently (e.g., MLA) to meet formatting standards.
Consistent page numbering continues to aid navigation across the extended works-cited list.
